title: Prohibit Password Reuse
description: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations.

checks.name: Password History is Enforced
checks.purpose: Verify Enforce password history is set to at least 24 passwords in the local or domain password policy

checks.name: Password History is Enforced
checks.purpose: Verify Enforce password history is set to at least 24 passwords in the domain password policy

checks.name: Fine-Grained Password Policy Enforces History for Privileged Accounts
checks.purpose: Confirm the Fine-Grained Password Policy for privileged accounts enforces at least 24 password history generations

checks.name: PAM Password History is Enforced
checks.purpose: Verify pam_pwhistory is configured in /etc/pam.d/common-password with remember=24 or higher to prevent password reuse

checks.name: PAM Password History is Enforced
checks.purpose: Verify pam_pwhistory is configured in /etc/pam.d/common-password with remember=24 or higher on Debian

remediation: Set Enforce password history to 24 passwords via GPO or domain password policy. On Linux, add pam_pwhistory.so remember=24 to /etc/pam.d/common-password. Configure Fine-Grained Password Policies to enforce the same history requirement for privileged accounts.
